_doc: Profiles for analyze_code_complexity.py. Selected with --profile <name>. Each profile sets exclude_dirs (path-segment names), exclude_globs (full-path glob patterns), ignore_macros, and fail_on thresholds. Explicit CLI flags override profile values. Profiles do NOT introduce git history, baselines, or cross-run state — the analyzer remains snapshot-only.

engine-ci.description: Production engine code only. Used by CheckComplexity.bat as a CI gate. Excludes games, tools, test fixtures, and vendor code so a wide gameplay dispatch or a UnitTests RunAllTests doesn't poison the gate. Test-only files (*.Tests.inl) are dropped via exclude_globs. Thresholds are checked-in ceilings of the current worst-case engine code, NOT aspirational targets — the gate keeps things from getting worse, not the definition of clean code.
engine-ci._gate_note: Architecture/lint gates are threshold-0: the per-finding allowlist files are the ratchet, not the number. Current findings are grandfathered in Tools/{layering,ecs_leaf,architecture,lints}_allowlist.txt; a NEW non-allowlisted finding fails. EXCEPTION: max-engine-singleton-count is a per-file COUNT ratchet (engine-wide g_xEngine reach, baseline Tools/engine_singleton_allowlist.txt, shrink-only) — see architecture.singleton_ratchet. Physics->Flux is now a tracked forbidden-edge (snapshotted in architecture_allowlist.txt; severed in Wave 1). Report-only (intentionally NOT gated): max-module-cycles (one giant engine-wide SCC), max-zone-of-pain-modules (foundational libs are intrinsically stable+concrete). max-static-violations IS gated (new module-scope mutable statics are a documented red flag; thread_local + named carve-outs are exempt). complexity.yml runs --gate-group complexity; layering-gate.yml runs --gate-group architecture,lints.
engine-ci._threshold_rationale: Bumped 2026-05-12 to clear the CI gate that had been red since 2026-05-07. New ceilings cover the current worst-case engine functions (Zenith_NavMesh::GetRandomReachablePointInRadius @ priority 82.5; Zenith_EditorAutomation::ExecuteAction @ CC 101 + LOC 1078; 5 duplicate clusters across Flux/AI). Per the engine-ci philosophy these are CEILINGS not targets; the next refactor that lowers a worst-case should lower the matching ceiling to keep the gate biting.
engine-ci._architecture_doc: Declarative module/layer DAG + ratchets. Subsumes Tools/layering_gate.ps1: the ec_flux_ratchet + leaf_ratchet reproduce its two checks (raw-string parity, same allowlist files). The resolved include graph drives the new full-DAG direction, encapsulation, coupling, hub and module-cycle checks. Layer ranks are a calibrated hypothesis — all current up-edges are grandfathered in the allowlists. fail_on_basename_resolved=false keeps low-confidence basename edges out of the gate.

singleton_ratchet._doc: Engine-wide g_xEngine reach as a per-file COUNT ratchet (shrink-only). Distinct from the leaf_ratchet (which gates g_xEngine to ZERO inside the ECS leaf only). Baseline counts in Tools/engine_singleton_allowlist.txt; a file's count may never exceed its baseline, and a new in-scope file (no entry => 0) fails. The composition root that legitimately wires the global (EngineComposition TUs + the engine header) is excluded. This is the measurement spine for the Wave-4 de-globalisation effort.

full-snapshot.description: Whole-repo health snapshot. Used by RunAnalysis.bat. Includes games, tools, and tests but excludes vendor/build directories. Informational only — never blocks CI. No fail-on thresholds.
